trivia_bullets.text: Undying Rage was first printed in Time Spiral (TSP, October 2006), a set defined by its timeshifted frame cards and explicit design homage to older Magic sets. The card's self-recurring mechanic echoes Time Spiral's preoccupation with cyclical, undying power. It has since appeared in Duel Decks: Heroes vs. Monsters and Commander Legends, totaling multiple printings.
No flavor text. Scryfall confirms the CMR printing of Undying Rage carries no flavor text; the card lets its art and name carry the emotional weight.

Undying Rage is an Aura that grants +2/+2 and forces the enchanted creature to attack (can't block), with built-in recursion: when it leaves the battlefield it returns to its owner's hand. This prevents the permanent two-for-one that kills most Auras, making it stickier than comparable pump Auras. Legal in Pauper and Modern; EDHREC rank 16863.

Scott M. Fischer has illustrated over 165 Magic cards since the mid-1990s. His portfolio site tracks original game art and artist proofs, making him one of the more commercially active legacy MTG illustrators in the secondary artist-proof market.
